evals.name: story-9x16-with-overlays
evals.prompt: I've got a screen recording of a product demo at test-recording.mp4 — it's 1920x1080, the left side (roughly x=10 to x=950) is a web browser and the right side (x=970 onward) is a chat/agent panel. Cut it down into a punchy ~15 second vertical clip for Instagram Stories (9:16), focusing on the browser side, and put a couple of text overlays on it. Give me the final mp4.
evals.expected_output: A 1080x1920 mp4, cut shorter than the source, cropped to the browser panel (not stretched), with legible text overlay(s) burned in. Overlays generated as PNGs via Pillow (this ffmpeg lacks drawtext).

evals.name: square-linkedin-clip
evals.prompt: Turn test-recording.mp4 (a 1920x1080 two-panel screen recording — browser on the left, agent chat on the right) into a square 1:1 clip I can post on LinkedIn. Keep it short, maybe speed up any slow parts, and add a title overlay at the top. Output the final video file.
evals.expected_output: A 1080x1080 mp4, shorter than 30s, with a centered crop (not squished), and a title overlay rendered via Pillow. Correct aspect ratio, even dimensions, H.264.

evals.name: vo-script-plus-shotlist
evals.prompt: I want to make a TikTok out of test-recording.mp4 — it's a screen recording, 1920x1080, browser on the left and an AI agent chat on the right. Before you render anything, write me a shot list and a voice-over script for a ~20 second vertical video. I'll approve it before you do the actual editing.
evals.expected_output: A markdown shot list table with per-segment timing/focus/overlay/VO columns, plus a clean standalone voice-over script (one line per segment, present tense). Should NOT render video yet — stops for approval.
